title: Raw config.json readers bypass the config migration layer — silent shape divergence once the first shape-changing config migration ships
description: Two readers parse config.json raw, skipping loadConfigForDir's migrate-then-validate: resolveOperationVocabulary (pi-agent-dispatch/src/capability-composer.ts:26, active substrate, fail-safe) and loadConfigForDirBestEffort (pi-context/src/context-sdk.ts:1443, non-active substrates). Harmless while only identity chains exist; the day a shape-changing `config` migration ships (map_each's declared consumer: the state_derivation delta), these see the un-migrated shape while loadConfig sees the migrated one — divergent reads of the same file, no throw, no signal. Class member of FGAP-105's raw-readers-cannot-tell-stale-from-current observation; distinct defect: these paths never migrate at all.

evidence.reference: resolveOperationVocabulary raw-parses the ACTIVE substrate's config.json (fail-safe try/catch), bypassing the migration-aware loader.
loadConfigForDirBestEffort raw-parses non-active substrates' config.json — best-effort by design, but shape-blind to migrations.

impact: Latent split-brain on config shape between the migration-aware and raw read paths; surfaces only when a non-identity config migration lands, far from this cause, with no error at the divergence point.
proposed_resolution: Requires determination: route both through the migration-aware loader (best-effort semantics preserved via try/catch) or document them as deliberately raw with a shape-floor contract. FGAP-105's write-time convergence narrows the exposure window (converged files carry the current shape on disk) but does not close it for lagging substrates.
